issue_snippets.description: Pickle and modules that wrap it can be unsafe when used to deserialize untrusted data, possible security issue.
issue_snippets.tool_snippet:
8     try:
9         payload = pickle.loads(base64.b64decode(request["payload"]))
10     except (pickle.UnpicklingError, ValueError) as e:

issue_snippets.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.fromstring with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issue_snippets.tool_snippet:
18     try:
19         config = ElementTree.fromstring(request["config"])
20     except (ElementTree.ParseError, ValueError) as e:
issue_snippets.context:
17:     # Parse config with ElementTree while disabling external entity resolution
    18:     try:
>>  19:         config = ElementTree.fromstring(request["config"])
    20:     except (ElementTree.ParseError, ValueError) as e:
    21:         return {"status": 400, "message": f"Invalid config: {e}"}
